name: dns-policy
title: GCP DNS — DNS Server Policy
summary: Creates a DNS server policy attached to one or more VPC networks: inbound forwarding (on-premises → Cloud DNS resolution), query logging, and optional network-wide alternative name servers.

governance.name: prefix
governance.description: Short organization or project prefix. First token of every resource name: {prefix}-{cloud}-{environment}-{resource-type}-{suffix}.

governance.name: environment
governance.description: Deployment environment identifier (e.g., dev, test, prod). Used in resource names.

governance.name: owner
governance.description: Team or individual that owns the deployed resources. Retained for convention parity (DNS policies are not labelable).

governance.name: cost_center
governance.description: Cost center code. Retained for convention parity (DNS policies are not labelable).

governance.name: additional_tags
governance.description: Retained for convention parity; DNS policies do not support labels.

governance.name: name_suffix
governance.description: Final token of every resource name, used to disambiguate multiple instances of this module.

module.name: project_id
module.description: GCP project ID owning the DNS policy.

module.name: network_self_links
module.description: Self-links of the VPC networks the policy attaches to. Only ONE DNS policy may exist per network — coordinate ownership.

module.name: enable_inbound_forwarding
module.description: Allocate inbound forwarder IPs in each attached network so on-premises resolvers can query Cloud DNS (GCP's analogue of an inbound resolver endpoint).

module.name: enable_logging
module.description: Enable query logging for the attached networks.

module.name: alternative_name_servers
module.description: Alternative name servers — ALL queries from attached networks (except Compute internal names) forward to these instead of Cloud DNS. Use sparingly; per-domain forwarding belongs in forwarding zones.

outputs.name: inbound_forwarding_enabled
outputs.description: Whether inbound forwarding is active. The allocated forwarder IPs are visible per network in the console/API (compute addresses named 'dns-forwarding-...'); GCP does not expose them as resource attributes.
